title: Processing-naturalness perturbation candidate
notes: Tenth-round mutation generated after agreement-attraction and center-embedding evaluations repeated the same missing mechanism: processing effects can perturb felt naturalness and reported acceptability without changing community licensing or grammaticality attribution. This graph is a scoped processing/measurement module, not a licensing account.

edges.rationale: Greater processing difficulty tends to lower felt naturalness in the judgment context.
Recoverable meaning or scaffolding can improve felt naturalness despite processing difficulty.
Naturalness reports and acceptability reports are linked, but the measurement is noisy and task-sensitive.
Elicitation format changes how felt naturalness is converted into a reported acceptability response.
Correctness, naturalness, speeded judgment, and comprehension framings can shift reported acceptability.
Task framing can cue whether respondents treat difficulty as a grammar verdict or as processing trouble.
Reported acceptability can partly measure licensing, but processing and task effects can mask that relation.
Licensing is evidence for attribution, not identical to the reported feeling of naturalness.
Acceptability reports provide contextual warrant for attribution but are not the target construct.

score_status.scope: Processing cost, felt naturalness, recoverability, measurement-task, acceptability, and attribution perturbation module; not a licensing account.
